lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: Ahoy, young whippersnapper. Have you come to hear a tale from old Ribs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me about Barnacle Barnaby?

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: He was a cruel and vicious pirate. Orchestrator of the Driftwood Massacre and unleasher of the Plague of Black Mists. He believed himself untouchable, but his arrogance was his undoing.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: Despite the warnings of many, Barnacle Barnaby attempted to plunder the Skullhorde, but his only reward was a deadly curse. It destroyed the Crimson Kraken, condemning all on board to a watery grave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you had a map leading to the wreck of the Kraken?

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: Aye! I purchased the map from Ulrich the Unfortunate, the sole survivor of the Kraken, but I had no intention of using it. I knew that no good would ever come from seeking the Kraken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then why not destroy the map?

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: And risk the wrath of Barnaby's ghost? No. I thought it better the map be locked away, so that neither it nor the curse could harm anyone else, but those young fools put an end to that.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: Now we can only hope that someone puts a stop to them before it's too late. Who knows, perhaps it will be you who does so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, on that note, I'd best be getting off. Until next time, Ribs.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: As you wish, but you would be wise to learn from your elders. My tales might one day save you from a one-way trip to see Davey Jones!
